steps.title: Identify the phrase structure
steps.content: The verb “eschew” is followed by the object “currency” and the prepositional phrase “in favor of a direct trade system”. These two parts work together to create a single, unified meaning: rejecting one thing in preference for another.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A prepositional phrase integral to a verb's meaning must remain directly connected to its object without intervening punctuation. Choice C correctly places “currency” and “in favor of a direct trade system” together without separating them. Options A, B, and D each insert a comma or dash between these parts, fragmenting the unified expression. That matches choice C — C is correct.
